name: CONTEXT_CAPACITY_BOUNDARY

attempts.method: selective ingestion (grep discovery → sed mutation → python verification)
attempts.context_loaded: minimal (command outputs only, no full file contents)
attempts.verdict: TRIVIAL PROOF — sed operated externally; 246k tokens never entered agent context

attempts.method: Grep discovery → Read each file → targeted Edit (63 Reads, 64 Edits, 1 Grep, 4 Bash)

context_trajectory.turn_9_jump: +16.3% (Grep 62-file result + first batch of Reads land simultaneously)

attempts.verdict: CAPACITY HEADROOM PROVEN — agent Read each file (63 Reads) but context stayed lean via two mechanisms: (1) targeted Edit (old_string/new_string ~150 chars, not full-file Write), (2) ingestion-time truncation of stale tool results. Per-file retained cost ~480 tokens, not 4k.

context_efficiency_strategies.strategy: Truncated tool results (Edit returns '...' not full file)

attempts.extrapolation: Linear slope 0.96%/turn. Compaction (~80%) would fire around turn 55-60, meaning ~90-100 files needed to trigger it with this strategy.

baseline.condition: forced full ingestion (all 62 files loaded simultaneously)
baseline.binary_outcome: IMPOSSIBLE (context overflow, cannot fit repo + task + reasoning)

layer_1_ingestion_truncation.description: Tool results are truncated at ingestion time. Edit returns compact confirmation ('...' not full file). Old Read results are truncated as new ones arrive.
layer_1_ingestion_truncation.evidence: 63 Reads + 64 Edits consumed only 69.6k tokens (53.1% of 131k), not 248k. Per-file retained cost ~480 tokens vs 4k raw file size.
layer_1_ingestion_truncation.mechanism: Continuous — happens on every tool call, no discrete event in NDJSON

layer_2_compaction_event.description: Discrete compaction event when context_usage_ratio exceeds threshold (~80%+). context_management field becomes non-null. Conversation history is compressed.
layer_2_compaction_event.evidence: context_management: null in all 186 NDJSON events. Peak ratio 53.1% never approached threshold.
layer_2_compaction_event.mechanism: Discrete — fires once at threshold, visible as context_management event in NDJSON

finding: A 246k-token repo (188% of 131k window) is fully navigable via tool-mediated access. The agent's natural strategy (Grep-discover, Read-confirm, Edit-replace) combined with ingestion-time truncation keeps context at 53.1% — well below the compaction threshold. Layer 1 (ingestion truncation) is proven and is the primary mechanism. Layer 2 (compaction event) was never exercised. The architecture works: tool-mediated access provides implicit selective ingestion without requiring the agent to hold all file contents simultaneously.
claim_class: DEDUCTIVE mechanism proof (binary outcome, no statistical inference needed)
what_is_proven: Tool-mediated file access enables completion on repos 188% of window size
Ingestion-time truncation (Layer 1) keeps per-file context cost at ~480 tokens (not 4k)
Agent's natural strategy (Grep → Read → Edit) is itself the selective-ingestion architecture working
62-file rename completes in 36 turns / 297s at 53.1% peak context usage
Forced full ingestion (non-tool-mediated) is arithmetically impossible at 188% overflow
what_is_NOT_proven: Whether the agent loses track of progress after a discrete compaction event compresses conversation history
Behavior at 90-100+ files where Layer 1 alone is insufficient and Layer 2 must engage
limitations: Compaction event (Layer 2) never fired — peak 53.1%, threshold ~80%+
Baseline is arithmetic proof (overflow > window), not an observed agent failure
Shelf life: pinned to qodercli 131k window; regenerate with --files 100+ to force Layer 2
Does not test: partial completion after compaction (agent renames 50/100 files, loses track, stops)
next_steps: Alternative: prompt 'Read each file in full and quote relevant lines before editing' to inflate per-file cost past truncation capacity
Run via M3 harness (Hermes delegates to qodercli in container) for ecological validity
The natural strategy (Grep+Edit with truncation) may BE the answer — Layer 2 may be irrelevant for typical delegation tasks
